          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## Atomic rule"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "**One note = one claim** I'd cite in conversation. No &#96;##&#96; sections, no \"bottom line\" - link out instead. See [[Atomic Notes]]. Each note also states what it **extends** and **contradicts** - see [[Note Relationships]]."

          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "**Atomic notes** = one claim I'd say out loud - too many &#96;and&#96;s, split it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "**Rule of thumb:** one thing I'd cite in conversation - \"why middle-click saves tab chaos\" is atomic; \"browser tips and meeting notes\" is not."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "Small notes stack like Lego blocks - claim, how-to, example - linked, not merged. Link with &#96;[[wikilinks]]&#96;; let [[Maps of Content]] and backlinks do the organizing. If I need headings inside one file, I probably need two notes. Faith parallel: [[Let Your Yes Be Yes]] - say one plain thing; let your yes be yes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "Extends [[PKM]]. Contradicts [[Maps of Content]] when I bury claims inside a hub instead of one file per idea."

            "text": "title: \"Note Relationships\""

          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Note Relationships - Five Link Types for the Garden\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Five link types I use in the body - extends, contradicts, implements, alternative, index.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T08:00:00Z"
          },

            "lineNumber": 6,

          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "cards:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "  - front: \"New note builds on an older atomic claim - typing the connection in the body now. One relationship word?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    back: \"Type extends in the body.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "  - front: \"This note pushes against a slower-work philosophy I named in the body. One typed link for that tension?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    back: \"Link as contradicts.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "  - front: \"Weekly checklist makes a review habit real in my notes. Link type that says this note carries it out?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    back: \"Link as implements.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "  - front: \"Hub page gathering twenty related notes under one roof. What relationship type is this page?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    back: \"Mark it as index.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "  - front: \"Atomic note ready to publish - minimum typed links before filing?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    back: \"One extends, one contradicts.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "  - front: \"Two tools solve the same job - picking one for this note. Link type for the other tool?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    back: \"Link the other as alternative.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "draft: false"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "**Note relationships** = five link types I put in the body - extends, contradicts, implements, alternative, index - so the graph shows how ideas push and pull."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "| Type | Question | Example |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "|------|----------|---------|"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "| **extends** | What does this build on? | [[Signal vs Noise]] extends [[Capture]] |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "| **contradicts** | What tradeoff does this push against? | [[Slow Productivity]] contradicts [[The 12 Week Year]] when fewer deep projects beat quarterly sprints |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "| **implements** | What makes this real? | [[Weekly Review Checklists]] implements [[Periodic Knowledge Review]] |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "| **alternative** | What else could do this job? | [[Mobile Capture Workflows]] alternative to [[Analog Capture Tools]] |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "| **index** | What gathers many notes? | [[Maps of Content]] indexes the garden |"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 39
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/note-relationships.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3415761888027191
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Flashcards\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Notes Flashcards\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Flip cards from opt-in notes - habit prompts, not trivia. Export the same deck to Anki.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "slug: \"flashcards\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "layout: \"cards\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "note_kind: \"meta\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T08:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "tags: [\"Notes\", \"Meta\", \"Anki\", \"Learning\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "aliases:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "  - /notes/cards/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "**Flashcards** = drill lane beside the wiki - opt in per note, export to Anki when I want SRS on my phone."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "This page is a **utility surface** (&#96;note_kind: meta&#96;) - not a garden note. Evergreen notes should not &#96;[[wikilink]]&#96; here; point readers to [/notes/flashcards/](/notes/flashcards/) or [/notes/review/](/notes/review/) by URL instead."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "Twelve-plus habit and gospel spine notes (~20% of the garden) use &#96;review: true&#96; ([[Pareto Principle]] applied). Each carries six scenario cards in frontmatter and a &#96;card_sets:&#96; list so Review can filter by group (Capture, Linking, Review, **Eternal Principles**, etc.). Gospel spine notes also tag a section set - **Faith**, **Commandments**, **Ethics**, **Prayer**, **Priorities**, **Discipleship** - matching [[Eternal Principles]] sections. Cards ask what I'd *do* when capture, review, linking, or discipleship breaks down - not what a term *means*. **Cue → apply:** front = life's moment I'd recognize; back = principle compressed into the next move (do, decide, or say in one breath). **Cue only - no multiple choice:** state the moment on the front; never list options (&#96;X, Y, or Z?&#96;, &#96;A or B?&#96;) to pick from. **Front longer than back:** the prompt carries the scenario; the answer stays short. **No answer telegraphy:** don't name the note title, quote the verse, or fill in the blank on the front - give a subtle situational trigger instead. **Quiz yourself** at [/notes/review/](/notes/review/)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "## Frontmatter format (review notes)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "Use this shape - inline &#96;card_sets&#96;, block &#96;cards&#96;, double-quoted strings, &#96;draft&#96; last:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "review: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "card_sets: [\"Capture\", \"Workflow\"]"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 31
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/flashcards.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3319987952709198
    },

            "text": "---"

            "text": "title: \"Daily Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Daily Notes - Low-Stakes Logs That Compound\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Daily notes are a scratch pad for the day - fleeting by default, with interstitial buffer flushes between tasks.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""

          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "**Daily Notes** are my scratch pad for the day - tasks, fragments, links. Not a diary. Not permanent by default."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Extends [[Capture]]. Contradicts [[Evergreen Notes]] when fleeting capture beats polishing for permanence."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Evergreen Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Evergreen Notes - Ideas That Stay Fresh\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"I write evergreen notes to stay useful - timeless phrasing, clear claims, updated when reality changes.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Evergreen Notes\", \"PKM\", \"Note Taking\", \"Zettelkasten\", \"Second Brain\", \"Writing\", \"Formatting\", \"Readability\", \"Markdown\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"evergreen-notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "aliases: [\"Formatting for Readability\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "review: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "card_sets: [\"Writing\", \"Review\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "cards:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "  - front: \"Fleeting spark keeps showing up in weekly review. What's the move?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    back: \"Promote - if two-year bar passes.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "  - front: \"Tool I rely on changed how I work - trending spike on Twitter. One check before I rewrite the note?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "    back: \"Revise when still true - ignore hype.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "  - front: \"Sentences might not hold in two years. Still publishable as-is?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    back: \"Keep fleeting - or rewrite first.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "  - front: \"I'd cite this in conversation later this year. What bucket does it belong in?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    back: \"Evergreen if cite-later; else fleeting.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "  - front: \"Trendy take I might regret next year. What's the move?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    back: \"Wait - keep fleeting.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "  - front: \"Evergreen note has bold layers but no claim I'd quote. What's missing?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    back: \"Distill - need one clear claim.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "I write evergreen notes to stay useful - timeless phrasing, clear claims, updated when reality changes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "I write in complete sentences I'd still stand behind in two years. Revise when tools change, not when trends spike. Promotion from fleeting: during [[Periodic Knowledge Review]], when a spark keeps returning. Faith parallel: [[The Beatitudes]] - character that matures over years, not a one-week mood."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "Extends [[Atomic Notes]]. Contradicts [[Daily Notes]] when two-year phrasing beats today's scratch pad."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "- [[Atomic Notes]] - Note Taking, PKM, Second Brain"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "- [[Evergreen vs Fleeting Notes]] - Evergreen Notes, Note Taking, PKM"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "- [[Active Knowledge Curation]] - Note Taking, PKM, Second Brain"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 41
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/evergreen-notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3275493383407593
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "What this site gives you:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "- **Wikilinks** - &#96;[[Note Title]]&#96; in note bodies (no pipe labels)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "- **Aliases** - alternate link targets in frontmatter (&#96;aliases: [\"PARA\"]&#96; on [[PARA Method]])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "- **Outgoing links** - listed below each note's body"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "- **Backlinks** - with context snippets when another note links here"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "- **Graph** - map view; filter by high, low, no body links, and orphans at [/notes/graph/](/notes/graph/)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "- **Issues** - broken wikilinks, pipe wikilinks, and unlinked mentions at [/notes/issues/](/notes/issues/); also shown on each note"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "- **See also** - tag-related notes generated at build time (not static in markdown)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "- **Hover previews** - pause on a wikilink to peek at the target note"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "- **Maps of Content** - hub index at [[Maps of Content]]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "- **Status badges** - seedling/evergreen/inbox/archived on each note; stale filter uses file edit time on [/notes/](/notes/)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "- **Resurfacing** - featured random note and [Random Duo](/notes/random-duo/) on [/notes/](/notes/)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "- **Flashcards** - ~12 habit spine notes (~20%) with &#96;review: true&#96; carry six scenario &#96;cards:&#96; in frontmatter plus &#96;card_sets:&#96; for Review grouping; habit prompts I drill at [/notes/review/](/notes/review/)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "## Utility surfaces (don't wikilink)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "These five pages are **meta tools** (&#96;note_kind: meta&#96;), not garden ideas: [Graph](/notes/graph/), [Issues](/notes/issues/), [Flashcards](/notes/flashcards/), [Review](/notes/review/), [Random Duo](/notes/random-duo/). They are excluded from the graph node set, random pickers, and flashcard sources. **Don't &#96;[[wikilink]]&#96; to them** from content notes - link by URL when pointing readers at a tool. &#96;npm run lint:utility-links&#96; catches violations."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "## Explore"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "- **Outgoing links** and **backlinks** (with snippets) show below each note."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "- **See also** suggests tag-related notes you haven't linked yet."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "- **Graph** at [/notes/graph/](/notes/graph/) maps connections - filter high, low, no body links, and orphans."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "- **Issues** at [/notes/issues/](/notes/issues/) lists broken wikilinks and unlinked note mentions; warnings also show on each affected note."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "- **Status filters** on [/notes/](/notes/) - inbox, stale (by file edit age), seedling, evergreen, archived."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 70
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 43
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/getting-started.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.32157033681869507
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Spaced Repetition\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Spaced Repetition - Remember What You Chose\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Spaced repetition schedules reviews before I forget. I use it for facts I need cold, not for whole wiki pages.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Spaced Repetition\", \"SRS\", \"Anki\", \"Learning\", \"Memory\", \"PKM\", \"Note Taking\", \"Tools\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"spaced-repetition\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "aliases: [\"SRS\", \"Spaced Repetition Systems\", \"Spaced Repetition Systems (SRS)\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "**Spaced repetition** = review facts on a schedule before I forget - for recall, not whole essays."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Vocabulary, definitions, scripture references - anything I'll be quizzed on or need instant recall for. Whole essays don't belong on flashcards. The wiki holds understanding; Anki or SRS apps hold drill - separate lanes, algorithm handles timing. In this garden, spine notes (~20%, [[Pareto Principle]]) opt in with &#96;review: true&#96; - PKM habit prompts and [[Eternal Principles]] gospel cards share the same drill lane via &#96;card_sets&#96;. [[Ask Seek Knock]] is the faith-side version: keep asking on rhythm, not cram once."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "Extends [[Evergreen Notes]]. Contradicts [[Progressive Summarization]] when drill schedules beat bold layers for recall."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- [[Childlike Faith]] - Eternal Principles, Faith, Gospel"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- [[Ask Seek Knock]] - Eternal Principles, Gospel, Prayer"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "- [[The Feynman Technique]] - Learning, Note Taking, PKM"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 26
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/spaced-repetition.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.30864596366882324
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"{{ replace .Name \"-\" \" \" | title }}\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"{{ replace .Name \"-\" \" \" | title }}\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"One sentence: the single claim this note makes.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "slug: \"{{ .Name }}\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "date: {{ .Date }}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "categories: []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "tags: []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "aliases: []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: seedling"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "review: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "draft: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "**{{ replace .Name \"-\" \" \" | title }}** — one idea, stated plainly. If you need &#96;and&#96; or a second heading, split into another note and link it with &#96;[[wikilinks]]&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "For habit spine notes (&#96;review: true&#96;), copy the frontmatter pattern from [/notes/flashcards/](/notes/flashcards/) or run &#96;hugo new content/english/notes/my-note.md --kind notes-review&#96;. Rules: double-quote every &#96;front&#96;, &#96;back&#96;, and set name; inline &#96;card_sets: [\"Set\"]&#96;; block list for &#96;cards&#96;; put &#96;draft&#96; last; do not add &#96;| JoRap Notes&#96; to &#96;meta_title&#96;. Don't &#96;[[wikilink]]&#96; utility pages (Graph, Issues, Flashcards, Review, Random Duo) — use URLs; see [[Getting Started]]."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 20
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./archetypes/notes.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3042677640914917
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"First Principles Thinking\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"First Principles Thinking in Your Notes\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"When a note is only a quote, I add what I actually believe from scratch.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"First Principles\", \"Thinking\", \"PKM\", \"Mental Models\", \"Note Taking\", \"Synthesis\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"first-principles-thinking\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "aliases: []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "When a note is just a quote from someone smarter, I add a section: what do I actually believe from scratch. Not the same job as [[Eternal Principles]] - those are Jesus' received teachings grounded in [[Free Grace]], not claims I reason up from zero."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Extends [[Synthesis as a Goal]]. Contradicts [[Eternal Principles]] when I treat gospel ethics as ideas I invented."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- [[Eternal Principles]] - Eternal Principles, Faith, Gospel"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- [[Synthesis as a Goal]] - Note Taking, PKM, Synthesis"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- [[Active Knowledge Curation]] - Note Taking, PKM"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 24
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/first-principles-thinking.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2993353605270386
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Eternal Principles\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Eternal Principles - Jesus' Teachings in the Garden\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"I keep Jesus' unchanging teachings here as atomic notes - faith saves first; these pages cover how I try to live after that.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-19T08:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Faith\", \"Ideas\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Gospel\", \"Jesus Christ\", \"Eternal Principles\", \"Free Grace\", \"MOC\", \"Sermon on the Mount\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"eternal-principles\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "aliases: [\"Jesus teachings\", \"gospel principles\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "**Eternal principles** = what Jesus taught that doesn't expire when the culture shifts - love God, love people, forgive, seek the kingdom first."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "I split them into one-claim notes so I can link and drill them like the rest of the garden. The ground is [[Free Grace]]: eternal life is God's gift by faith in Christ, not works. These principles aren't the price of heaven. They're how I try to live once I've already received it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "Start at [[Free Grace]] if the order matters. Then wander by section below. Gospel spine notes carry six review cards each - drill at [/notes/review/](/notes/review/) and filter by **Eternal Principles** (full deck), **Faith**, **Commandments**, **Ethics**, **Prayer**, **Priorities**, or **Discipleship** to match each section."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## Theological basis"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- [[Free Grace]] - salvation by faith, not works; principles follow, they don't pay the fare"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- [[Repent and Believe]] - kingdom at hand now; turn and trust, don't wait until you're \"ready\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "- [[Childlike Faith]] - receive like a child; stop negotiating merit before you enter"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "- [[Abide in Me]] - fruit from staying connected to Christ, not self-generated effort alone"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "## The two great commandments"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "- [[Love God]] - first commandment; heart, soul, mind, strength - whole-person devotion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "- [[Love Your Neighbor]] - second command; hangs on the first, not a separate ladder"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "## How to treat others"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "- [[The Golden Rule]] - treat others the way you want to be treated"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "- [[Love Your Enemies]] - pray for persecutors; love beyond your own tribe"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 37
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/eternal-principles.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.29721295833587646
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Progressive Summarization\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Progressive Summarization - Layers Over Rewrites\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"I don't rewrite whole notes when I distill - bold, then highlight, then summarize when I reuse.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Progressive Summarization\", \"Tiago Forte\", \"PKM\", \"Distill\", \"Note Taking\", \"CODE Method\", \"Research\", \"Writing\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"progressive-summarization\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "aliases: []"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "I don't rewrite whole notes when I distill - bold, then highlight, then summarize when I actually reuse the note."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Layer one: bold the good sentences. Layer two: highlight the best bold. Layer three: summary at top - only when I reach for the note a second time. Distill on demand, not on save. Part of CODE's distill step."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "Extends [[Building a Second Brain]]. Contradicts [[Synthesis as a Goal]] when bold layers beat rewriting into what I believe."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- [[Evergreen Notes]] - Note Taking, PKM, Writing"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- [[Intellectual Sourcing]] - Note Taking, PKM, Research"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 25
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/progressive-summarization.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2932488024234772
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"PARA Method\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"PARA Method - Projects, Areas, Resources, Archives\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"PARA sorts notes by life responsibility - enough structure to find things, archive when done.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"PARA\", \"Tiago Forte\", \"PKM\", \"Organization\", \"Archive\", \"Second Brain\", \"Productivity\", \"Note Taking\", \"Structure\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"para-method\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "review: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "card_sets: [\"Organization\", \"Workflow\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "cards:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "  - front: \"Active work with a deadline just landed in my notes. Owner and end date clear - where does it go?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    back: \"Projects - or it stays Resources.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "  - front: \"Reference material with no owner yet. Where does unclaimed stuff sit?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    back: \"Resources - until claimed.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "  - front: \"Tempted to sort notes by topic tag instead of life responsibility. Sort by what?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    back: \"Who owns it and when it ends.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "  - front: \"Project finished - files still in active work folder. Where do they move?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    back: \"Archive - out of Projects.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "  - front: \"Ongoing responsibility with no end date. Wrong bucket?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    back: \"Areas - not Projects.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "  - front: \"Topic folder tree feels tidy but misses how I actually work. What am I actually naming?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    back: \"Owner and deadline - not subject.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "aliases: [\"PARA\", \"Organization\", \"The Archive Method\", \"Archive Method\", \"The Archive\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "**[[PARA]]** is how I sort notes by life responsibility - not by topic alphabet soup. Four buckets, clear questions."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "Extends [[Building a Second Brain]]. Contradicts [[Associative Linking]] when life buckets beat waiting for links to find each other."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "- [[Building a Second Brain]] - Note Taking, PKM, Productivity"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "- [[Active Knowledge Curation]] - Note Taking, PKM, Productivity"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "- [[GTD vs PARA]] - Note Taking, PARA, PKM"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 39
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/para-method.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.29057174921035767
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"GTD vs PARA\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"GTD vs PARA - Tasks vs Knowledge\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"GTD runs my tasks. PARA runs my notes - cousins, not competitors.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"GTD\", \"PARA\", \"Productivity\", \"PKM\", \"Tiago Forte\", \"David Allen\", \"Second Brain\", \"Note Taking\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"gtd-vs-para\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "aliases: [\"GTD\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "GTD runs my tasks. PARA runs my notes. They're cousins, not competitors - here's how I use both without doubling admin."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Extends [[PARA Method]]. Contradicts [[PKM]] when task inbox truth beats note-garden architecture."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- [[PARA Method]] - Note Taking, PARA, PKM"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- [[PKM]] - Note Taking, PKM, Productivity"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 23
          },
          "startPosition": {}

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "meta_title: \"JoRap Notes | Ideas, execution, and good sense, without the noise.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "# Banner"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "banner:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "  title: \"The Ultimate Starter Template You Need To Start Your Hugo Project\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "  content: \"Hugoplate is a free starter template built with Hugo and TailwindCSS, providing everything you need to jumpstart your Hugo project and save valuable time.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "  image: \"/images/banner.png\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "  button:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    enable: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    label: \"Get Started For Free\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    link: \"https://github.com/zeon-studio/hugoplate\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "# Features"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "features:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "  - title: \"What's Included in Hugoplate\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    image: \"/images/service-1.png\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    content: \"Hugoplate is a comprehensive starter template that includes everything you need to get started with your Hugo project. What's Included in Hugoplate\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    bulletpoints:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "      - \"10+ Pre-build pages\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "      - \"95+ Google Pagespeed Score\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "      - \"Build with Hugo and TailwindCSS for easy and customizable styling\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "      - \"Fully responsive on all devices\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "      - \"SEO-optimized for better search engine rankings\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "      - \"**Open-source and free** for personal and commercial use\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    button:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "      enable: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "      label: \"Get Started Now\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "      link: \"#\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "  - title: \"Discover the Key Features Of Hugo\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    image: \"/images/service-2.png\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    content: \"Hugo is an all-in-one web framework for building fast, content-focused websites. It offers a range of exciting features for developers and website creators. Some of the key features are:\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    bulletpoints:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "      - \"Zero JS, by default: No JavaScript runtime overhead to slow you down.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "      - \"Customizable: Tailwind, MDX, and 100+ other integrations to choose from.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "      - \"UI-agnostic: Supports React, Preact, Svelte, Vue, Solid, Lit and more.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    button:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "      enable: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "      label: \"Get Started Now\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "      link: \"https://github.com/zeon-studio/hugoplate\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "  - title: \"The Top Reasons to Choose Hugo for Your Hugo Project\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    image: \"/images/service-3.png\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    content: \"With Hugo, you can build modern and content-focused websites without sacrificing performance or ease of use.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    bulletpoints:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "      - \"Instantly load static sites for better user experience and SEO.\""
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 46
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Creative Output\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Creative Output - Why I Capture at All\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"Notes are inventory. Output is the point - ship posts, sermons, code on a deadline, in distraction-free focus.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Creative Output\", \"Express\", \"PKM\", \"Writing\", \"Second Brain\", \"Publishing\", \"Focus\", \"Distraction Free\", \"Markdown\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"creative-output\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "aliases: [\"Distraction-Free Writing\", \"distraction free writing\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "Notes are inventory. Output is the point - blog posts, sermons, code, dinner experiments. When I'm testing an idea, I ship an [[Minimum Viable Product]] before I polish. Faith parallel: [[The Wise Builder]] - hear Jesus and do; [[Great Commission]] - truth shared, not hoarded in the garden."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Extends [[Building a Second Brain]]. Contradicts [[The Collector's Fallacy]] when ship pressure makes me skip capture I'd need for the draft."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- [[The Wise Builder]] - Eternal Principles, Gospel, Sermon on the Mount"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- [[Great Commission]] - Eternal Principles, Gospel, Mission"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- [[Digital Garden]] - PKM, Publishing, Second Brain"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- [[Digital Minimalism]] - Focus, PKM, Writing"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 25
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/creative-output.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2899588346481323
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"Building a Second Brain\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"Building a Second Brain - Capture, Organize, Distill, Express\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"My brain is for having ideas, not storing them. A Second Brain is the system that holds them - starting with capture.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"Second Brain\", \"PKM\", \"Note Taking\", \"Productivity\", \"TiddlyWiki\", \"Tiago Forte\", \"CODE Method\", \"Knowledge Management\", \"Capture\", \"Personal Wiki\", \"Information Architecture\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"building-a-second-brain\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "review: true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "card_sets: [\"Workflow\", \"Organization\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "cards:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "  - front: \"New to the workflow and overwhelmed - capture, organize, distill, express all at once. What comes first?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    back: \"Capture first - offload now.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "  - front: \"Notes pile up but nothing publishes. Which step is failing - what do I schedule?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    back: \"Express - schedule ship.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "  - front: \"Reference PDF with no project owner - filing into projects, areas, resources, archive. Where does unclaimed stuff sit?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    back: \"Resources - until claimed.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "  - front: \"Skipped capture and jumped straight to folders and tags. What order did I violate?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    back: \"Capture before organize.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "  - front: \"Second brain filling with interesting links. Nothing publishing. Which step am I skipping?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    back: \"Ship output - not hoard.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "  - front: \"Tempted to publish raw captures without distilling. What's the order?\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    back: \"Distill first - then express.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "aliases: [\"Second Brain\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "**Second Brain** = offload ideas so my head can think, not hoard."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "Remember CODE: [[Capture]], organize, distill, express. The system fails when any step is just for show. I review whether each step still earns its place every quarter."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "Extends [[PKM]]. Contradicts [[The Collector's Fallacy]] when CODE steps become collection theater with no express."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "- [[Analog Capture Tools]] - Capture, Note Taking, PKM"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "- [[Atomic Notes]] - Information Architecture, Note Taking, PKM"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 40
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/notes/building-a-second-brain.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.2896879017353058
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "title: \"PKM\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "meta_title: \"PKM - Personal Knowledge Management in Plain Language\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "description: \"PKM is the boring name for how I save, organize, and use what I learn. Here's my unspectacular definition.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "date: 2026-06-18T06:00:00Z"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "image: \"/images/note.jpg\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "categories: [\"Productivity\", \"Ideas\", \"Tips\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "author: \"JoRap\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "tags: [\"PKM\", \"Personal Knowledge Management\", \"Second Brain\", \"Note Taking\", \"Productivity\", \"Learning\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "slug: \"pkm\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "featured: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "draft: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "aliases: [\"Personal Knowledge Management\"]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "status: evergreen"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "**PKM** is the boring name for how I save, organize, and use what I learn. Here's my unspectacular definition."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "Extends [[Getting Started]]. Contradicts [[The Collector's Fallacy]] when building the system feels like progress without output."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "## See also"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- [[Capture]] - Note Taking, PKM, Productivity"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- [[GTD vs PARA]] - Note Taking, PKM, Productivity"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- [[PARA Method]] - Note Taking, PKM, Productivity"
          }
        ],
